# protonpass_alias (Resource)

Creates a Hide-My-Email Alias in Proton Pass.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "protonpass_alias" "example" {
  share_id            = protonpass_vault.example.share_id
  prefix              = "netflix_signup"
  destroy_permanently = true
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `prefix` (String) The prefix of the email alias (e.g., `my_prefix` results in `my_prefix.random@passmail.net`).
- `share_id` (String) The Share ID of the vault where the alias is created.

### Optional

- `destroy_permanently` (Boolean) If true, the alias is permanently deleted on destroy instead of moved to trash.

### Read-Only

- `alias` (String) The fully generated email address alias provided by Proton Pass.
- `id` (String) The unique identifier of the alias item.

## Import

Import is supported using the following syntax:

The [`terraform import` command](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/cli/commands/import) can be used, for example:

```shell
# Aliases can be imported by specifying the Vault Share ID and Item ID separated by a colon
terraform import protonpass_alias.example "share_id:item_id"
```
